title: P0: Fix rampant inventory failures — empty inventory on spawn and stale guard not waiting
description:
Inventory has been consistently reported as empty across multiple sprints, even when the bot has items (creative mode, /give, prior gathering). Three root causes fixed in Sprint 57:

1. **No inventory on spawn**: The adapter's `bot.once('spawn')` handler sent position/hp/food but NOT inventory. Fix: `sendBotStatus()` is now called on spawn to send full inventory immediately.

2. **GetStatus at end of plan**: The planner placed GetStatus as the LAST action after 200+ gather/build actions. All those actions ran with empty/stale inventory. Fixed by the stale-guard change below.

3. **Stale guard didn't wait**: The `IsInventoryStale` guard enqueued GetStatus then proceeded to plan generation on the next loop iteration because the guard only fired when stale AND no GetStatus pending. Once GetStatus was pending, the guard was skipped entirely, and the code fell through to PlanAsync. Fix: The guard now has two branches — (a) no GetStatus pending → enqueue one, (b) GetStatus pending → keep waiting. The loop continues (doesn't fall through) until IsInventoryStale becomes false.

The adapter also sends full inventory on spawn (`sendBotStatus()` call in spawn handler), so initial state is correct without needing an explicit GetStatus round-trip.
